name: New Delhi Spiced Chicken
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt 2 tablespoons butter or margarine in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the first six ingredients and stir to combine. Cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
2. Prepare chicken breasts by removing bones and skin, then cutting into bite-sized cubes.
Add chicken to the skillet with the spices. Stir to coat evenly, then cook for an additional
minute or until the chicken turns white on the inside when pierced.
3. Reduce heat to medium-low. Add remaining butter and sauté onions until they become limp.
Gradually add tomatoes, green bell peppers, celery, parsley, garlic, raisins, dry sherry,
and lemon juice. Stir well to combine all ingredients evenly.
4. Return chicken to the skillet and simmer gently for about 20-25 minutes or until
vegetables are tender but still slightly crisp. Adjust seasoning with black pepper if needed.
5. Serve over cooked brown rice.
